en: A PRAYER OF ONE AFFLICTED, WHEN HE IS FAINT AND POURS OUT HIS COMPLAINT BEFORE THE LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD; let my cry come to you!
zh: 耶和華啊！求你聽我的禱告； 願我的呼求達到你那裡。

en: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress! Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call!
zh: 我在患難的日子， 求你不要向我掩面； 我呼求的時候， 求你留心聽我，並且迅速應允我。

en: For my days pass away like smoke, and my bones burn like a furnace.
zh: 因為我的年日好像煙一般消散； 我的骨頭燒焦像爐中的炭。

en: My heart is struck down like grass and has withered; I forget to eat my bread.
zh: 我的心受創傷， 好像草一般枯乾， 以致我連飯也忘了吃。

en: Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
zh: 因我唉哼的聲音， 我就漸漸消瘦 。

en: I am like a desert owl of the wilderness, like an owl of the waste places;
zh: 我像曠野的鵜鶘， 又像廢墟裡的貓頭鷹。

en: I lie awake; I am like a lonely sparrow on the housetop.
zh: 我躺在床上警醒著， 就像屋頂上孤單的麻雀。

en: All the day my enemies taunt me; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
zh: 我的仇敵終日辱罵我； 嘲弄我的指著我賭咒。

en: For I eat ashes like bread and mingle tears with my drink,
zh: 我吃爐灰像吃飯一樣； 我所喝的與眼淚混和。

en: because of your indignation and anger; for you have taken me up and thrown me down.
zh: 這都是因為你的憤恨和烈怒； 你把我舉起來，又把我摔下去。

en: My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
zh: 我的年日好像日影偏斜， 我也好像草一般枯乾。

en: But you, O LORD, are enthroned forever; you are remembered throughout all generations.
zh: 耶和華啊！你卻永遠坐著為王， 你可記念的名也必存到萬代。

en: You will arise and have pity on Zion; it is the time to favor her; the appointed time has come.
zh: 你要起來，憐憫錫安， 因為現在是恩待錫安的時候； 所定的日期已經到了。

en: For your servants hold her stones dear and have pity on her dust.
zh: 因為你的眾僕人喜愛它的石頭， 愛惜它的塵土。

en: Nations will fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth will fear your glory.
zh: 萬國都必敬畏耶和華的名； 世上列王都敬畏你的榮耀。

en: For the LORD builds up Zion; he appears in his glory;
zh: 因為耶和華必建造錫安， 在他自己的榮耀裡顯現。

en: he regards the prayer of the destitute and does not despise their prayer.
zh: 他要垂顧困苦人的禱告， 必不藐視他們的祈求。

en: Let this be recorded for a generation to come, so that a people yet to be created may praise the LORD:
zh: 這些事要記下來，傳給後代， 好使將來出生的人民可以讚美耶和華。

en: that he looked down from his holy height; from heaven the LORD looked at the earth,
zh: 因為耶和華從他至高的聖所裡垂看， 從天上觀看大地，

en: to hear the groans of the prisoners, to set free those who were doomed to die,
zh: 為要垂聽被囚的人的歎息， 解救定了死罪的人；

en: that they may declare in Zion the name of the LORD, and in Jerusalem his praise,
zh: 使人在錫安傳揚耶和華的名， 在耶路撒冷傳揚讚美他的話。

en: when peoples gather together, and kingdoms, to worship the LORD.
zh: 那時，萬民和萬國必一同聚集， 要事奉耶和華。

en: He has broken my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
zh: 耶和華在我壯年的時候，使我的力量衰弱， 縮短我的年日。

en: "O my God," I say, "take me not away in the midst of my days- you whose years endure throughout all generations!"
zh: 所以我說：“我的 神啊！ 求你不要使我中年去世， 你的年數萬代無疆。

en: Of old you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
zh: 你起初立了地的根基； 天也是你手的工作。

en: They will perish, but you will remain; they will all wear out like a garment. You will change them like a robe, and they will pass away,
zh: 天地都要毀滅，你卻長存； 天地都要像衣服一樣漸漸殘舊； 你要更換天地如同更換衣物一樣， 天地就被更換。

en: but you are the same, and your years have no end.
zh: 只有你永不改變， 你的年數也沒有窮盡。

en: The children of your servants shall dwell secure; their offspring shall be established before you.
zh: 你僕人的子孫必安然居住； 他們的後裔必在你面前堅立。”
